question: Spec bond→tool permission mapping for the Pi harness extension. Read ~/.local/share/koad-io/harnesses/pi/extensions/koad-io-bond-gate.ts (the strawman) and /home/koad/.vesta/briefs/2026-05-18-bond-gated-tool-access.md (the brief). Write a spec that defines: (1) bond file format, (2) bond type → tool permission mapping, (3) discovery path, (4) audit emission type, (5) default scope when no bonds exist.
answer: Covered by VESTA-SPEC-200 (filed 2026-05-22). (1) Bond format: SPEC-200 §2 + SPEC-055 — capabilities field on authorization bond frontmatter. (2) Bond type→tool mapping: SPEC-200 §4.1 — default capability matrix per bond type. (3) Discovery: SPEC-200 §8 — harness reads all active bonds from ~/.<entity>/trust/bonds/. (4) Audit emission: SPEC-200 §8.4 — tool.blocked event with feature, reason, bond source. (5) Default scope: SPEC-200 §4 — bond type defaults apply; no bonds = own directory readonly with secrets blocked.
